message:
fix(telemetry): Histogram fails closed on NaN samples and NaN quantiles (ft-b4l62)

[MEDIUM] Two silent-wrong-answer paths in `Histogram` from one
underlying gotcha: f64 arithmetic does not sanitise NaN inputs.

## Defect 1: NaN sample poisons `total_sum` permanently

`record(NaN)` executed `self.total_sum += NaN`. NaN is absorbing
under addition, so `total_sum` became NaN forever — `mean()` then
returned `Some(NaN)` on every subsequent call regardless of how many
clean samples followed. Dashboards exporting the running mean would
surface NaN for the life of the process.

## Defect 2: NaN quantile silently returns the smallest sample

`quantile(NaN)` relied on `q.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` to validate `q`. But
`f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN (documented Rust behaviour), and
`(NaN as usize)` is 0. So a NaN quantile landed on `sorted[0]` and
returned `Some(smallest_sample)` — a plausible-looking number that
had no relationship to the requested quantile.

## Fix

Both follow the ft-761tz (disk_pressure) / ft-7ojbj (rate_limit
Retry-After) fail-closed precedent:

* `Histogram::record` — `if value.is_nan() { return; }` before any
  mutation. NaN is dropped on input; count, sum, min/max, sample
  window all stay clean.
* `Histogram::quantile` — `if self.samples.is_empty() || q.is_nan()
  { return None; }`. NaN `q` is now an explicit `None`, not a
  plausible-looking wrong answer.

## Tests (4 new)

* ft_b4l62_histogram_record_nan_does_not_poison_mean — pins that one
  NaN in a 10/20/NaN/30 stream yields mean=20.0 (not NaN), count=3
  (not 4), retained=3.
* ft_b4l62_histogram_all_nan_keeps_mean_none — all-NaN stream
  surfaces as "no samples recorded" rather than silently-NaN mean.
* ft_b4l62_histogram_quantile_nan_returns_none — NaN `q` → None.
* ft_b4l62_histogram_quantile_infinity_inputs_still_work — regress
  that the `clamp` fallback behaviour for ±inf (clamp to 0.0 or 1.0)
  is preserved after the NaN guard is added.

## Verification

`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` against
a fresh `/tmp/ft-rv-p3-target` — lib compiles clean (189 pre-existing
warnings, no new ones). The lib-test surface is blocked by a
pre-existing `CompatRuntime::block_on` trait-not-in-scope error in
`workflows/handlers.rs:7263` that belongs to another pane's
in-progress migration; that is outside ft-b4l62's scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
